opp.name: Coursera_contract+eproc+intake_Mar26
opp.next_step: Send Gartner Magic Quadrant report Send NDA Arrange demo session in March Provide post-call reading materials for review
opp.ais_why:
• The 19-minute intro covered Zycus's 'AI-driven automation' pitch, and Coursera's team asked how intake integrates with purchase requests via Teams or Slack - interest in orchestration, not specifically AI.
• Coursera flagged misalignment on the call purpose (expected a demo) and is gating ERP details behind an NDA; questions stayed mechanical rather than aspirational on AI.
• One recorded intro plus a cancelled follow-up; next demo deferred to March - engagement is steady but AI excitement is Zycus-led, so Curious.

items.source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query; Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026 attendees

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Emily Karjala
Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu
metrics.narrative: Coursera articulated operational pain around procurement elegance and workflow orchestration but provided no quantified impact. Emily Karjala (Senior Vendor Manager) stated on 29 Jan 2026 that 'we have systems now, ways of doing things, but it's not always the most elegant,' signaling dissatisfaction with current state but no cost, cycle-time, or ROI baseline. The business problem centers on intake-to-contract-to-PO workflow complexity: Elaine Gu asked 'How do you orchestrate all these pieces?' and 'I do a contract and then automatically create a intake... or is it intake first, contract then and then PO?' indicating fragmented process pain. No compelling event, no quantified savings target, no financial pain owner identified. The deal is discovery-stage with qualitative pain articulated but no business case built. Next step: quantify the pain (FTE burden, approval cycle time, maverick spend leakage) and tie it to a financial outcome the Economic Buyer would sponsor.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, attendee behavior
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Elaine Gu (Procurement Director) showed the strongest engagement on 29 Jan 2026, articulating evaluation criteria and requesting a demo of intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration, but provided no evidence of internal advocacy, EB access, or competitive blocking. She deflected questions about Coupa status and process, signaling caution or political complexity, not championship. Sumesh Sekaran (Director, Vendor Management & Global Sourcing) characterized the engagement as 'kicking the tires' and stated he was unavailable until March, suggesting low urgency and no active sponsorship. Emily Karjala (Senior Vendor Manager) participated but did not drive the agenda. All three went silent after the 29 Jan call with no follow-up, no inbound requests, no champion behavior. Zero OpportunityContactRole entries on the opp (hygiene gap) and no subsequent engagement in 101 days confirms no champion exists. The deal stalled because no one internally is selling for Zycus. Next move: re-engage Elaine and Sumesh to assess current interest, and if genuine, develop Elaine into a champion by arming her with a business case (quantified pain, ROI model, executive storyline) and securing her commitment to introduce the Economic Buyer and drive the evaluation forward.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu deflection on Coupa status
competition.narrative: No competitor identified. Coursera uses Coupa today for procure-to-pay. When asked if the Coupa contract was coming up or if there was 'Coupa fatigue,' Elaine Gu responded on 29 Jan 2026: 'I don't think we have any. There's can't share other things yet,' deflecting the question and signaling either procurement sensitivity, an undisclosed evaluation, or political complexity. Whether this is a Coupa replacement play, a coexistence scenario (Zycus for intake/contract, Coupa for P2P), or a future-state exploration is unknown. No other vendor named in any source (Competitors__c empty, no Avoma mention, no Next_Step reference, no Task). The deal is pre-competitive: no RFP, no shortlist, no bake-off. Inferred risk: if Coursera is exploring Zycus as a Coupa replacement, the 101-day silence may signal Coupa renewal closed the window. If coexistence was the play, integration complexity may have stalled it. The competitive read is a gap, not because competition is absent, but because the buyer withheld it and the deal went dark before discovery deepened. Next move: re-engage and explicitly ask whether Coupa contract status has changed, whether other vendors are being evaluated, and whether Zycus is being considered as a replacement, complement, or future option. This is the single most important qualification question to answer before investing further.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Emily Karjala
Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu
identify_pain.narrative: Coursera articulated operational pain around procurement workflow fragmentation and lack of elegance in current systems. Emily Karjala stated on 29 Jan 2026: 'We have systems now, ways of doing things, but it's not always the most elegant,' indicating friction in the procure-to-pay process. Elaine Gu's repeated questions about intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration ('How do you orchestrate all these pieces?' and 'I do a contract and then automatically create a intake... or is it intake first, contract then and then PO?') reveal confusion and manual handoffs in the current workflow. The pain is confirmed but qualitative only, not quantified. Coursera uses Coupa today; Elaine deflected when asked about contract status or 'Coupa fatigue,' saying 'can't share other things yet,' implying either renewal pressure, dissatisfaction, or competitive exploration underway. Pain owner: Elaine Gu (Procurement Director, business owner of procure-to-pay tool) is the articulated pain owner. The pain is real but undeveloped: no cost of status quo, no FTE burden quantified, no cycle-time baseline, no executive-level pain validation. Next move: quantify the pain with Elaine and Emily (manual effort, approval delays, maverick spend, Coupa gaps) and escalate the business case to the Economic Buyer (COO or CFO) to convert operational friction into a funded initiative.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu NDA request
Salesforce account contacts (Blair MaryBeth, Anne Cappel General Counsel)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process unknown. On 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu requested an NDA before disclosing ERP details ('for those information, we probably need to sign an NDA'), and Zycus committed to sending it (logged in Next_Step__c: 'Send NDA'). No evidence the NDA was executed or even sent. No discussion of MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement contracting mechanics. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date populated in Salesforce to anchor a forcing function. The deal stalled in pre-NDA stage, so contracting mechanics remain unexplored. Next move: send the NDA immediately upon re-engagement, execute it, then map the procurement and legal review process (approval gates, InfoSec requirements, contracting vehicle, and procurement lead counsel). The account roster shows Blair MaryBeth (Director, Legal Operations & Procurement Lead Counsel) as a key contact for paper process discovery.

sources: Salesforce account-level contacts (not opp contact roles)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not identified. The 29 Jan 2026 discovery call included three practitioners: Emily Karjala (Senior Vendor Manager), Sumesh Sekaran (Director, Vendor Management & Global Sourcing), and Elaine Gu (Procurement Director under finance). Elaine leads the central procurement org and owns the procure-to-pay tool, making her the likely business buyer, but no commercial or budget conversation occurred on the call. The Salesforce account roster shows COO Shravan Goli and CFO (account-level contact) as potential Economic Buyers, but neither appeared on the discovery call and no contact roles are logged on this opp. No evidence of who controls the budget, no pricing discussion, no procurement authority confirmed. This is a fatal gap for a deal 148 days into Qualified stage. Next move: map the budget authority and secure a direct conversation with the Economic Buyer (likely the COO or CFO given the $130k size and enterprise procurement scope) to validate business case and commercial feasibility.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Sumesh Sekaran
Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu
decision_process.narrative: Decision process unknown. The 29 Jan 2026 call was an introductory session, not a structured evaluation. Sumesh Sekaran stated he was 'kicking the tires' when asked if there was a project, indicating no formal procurement initiative at that time. No RFP timeline, no approval chain discussed, no vendor shortlist confirmed. Elaine Gu mentioned she 'can't share other things yet' when asked about Coupa contract status, implying procurement sensitivity or an undisclosed process. Coursera uses Coupa today; whether this is a replacement evaluation, a coexistence play, or a future-state exploration is unclear. The call sequence (single discovery call followed by a cancelled second meeting and 101 days of silence) suggests no active decision process. No evidence of procurement approvals, legal review gates, or decision timeline. Next move: re-engage Sumesh and Elaine to confirm whether a formal procurement project has been initiated in the interim, and if so, map the decision process (evaluation stages, approval gates, decision committee, and timeline). If no project exists, qualify whether Coursera has budget and urgency or remains in exploratory mode.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu quotes
decision_criteria.narrative: Elaine Gu articulated three evaluation priorities on 29 Jan 2026: (1) intake integration with purchase request workflow via Teams or Slack, (2) linkage between intake, contract management, and PO generation ('how do you orchestrate all these pieces?'), and (3) demonstration of the end-to-end flow ('intake first, contract then and then PO?'). The buyer is evaluating workflow automation and system integration depth, not just feature presence. Elaine requested to 'see that piece' (intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration) as the centerpiece of any demo. No formal RFP, no weighted scorecard, no competitive bake-off criteria confirmed. The deal stalled before the demo that would have validated fit against these criteria. Inferred: procurement operational efficiency, ERP integration capability (NDA required before disclosing ERP), and AI-driven intake/contract automation are implicit criteria given the product scope (iContract, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake). Next step: deliver the demo Elaine requested, walking the intake-to-contract-to-PO workflow with Coursera's collaboration tool and ERP in view, and confirm whether Zycus meets the three stated priorities.

However, AIS_Score__c and AIS_Status__c are unpopulated in Salesforce, so the formal AI excitement read is unknown. Elaine Gu's questions about intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration align with Merlin's AI-driven workflow automation value prop. The deal stalled before AI fit could be validated in a demo. Inferred tier: AI Curious based on stated interest in intake automation and Merlin product scope, but unconfirmed.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created and qualified 29 Jan 2026 following introductory discovery call same day. Zycus committed to four immediate follow-ups (Gartner report, NDA, demo scheduling, reading materials) but no evidence of delivery. A second meeting scheduled for 23 Feb 2026 was cancelled with no reschedule. LastActivityDate 17 Mar 2026 (101 days ago) marks the last known touch; no buyer engagement since. The deal has been dark for over three months with no forward movement, no completed commitments, no demo delivered, and no buyer outreach. Recent Salesforce activity (tasks logged 17 Mar referencing Sumesh, Ramona Moritz, Elaine Gu, Sumesh Sekaran) appears to be administrative or retrospective logging, not live buyer engagement. The deal is effectively stalled in pre-demo stage.

items.detail: Economic Buyer not identified or engaged after 148 days in Qualified stage. No budget conversation, no commercial discussion, no EB sponsorship. COO and CFO unmapped. Fatal gap for a $130k enterprise deal.
All four immediate commitments undelivered: Gartner Magic Quadrant report, NDA, demo scheduling, post-call reading materials. Execution failure blocked forward momentum and credibility with buyer.

Coupa incumbency and undisclosed procurement dynamics. Elaine Gu deflected questions about Coupa contract status ('can't share other things yet'), signaling political sensitivity or competitive complexity. Whether Coupa renewed and closed the window is unknown.

No formal project or procurement timeline. Sumesh stated 'kicking the tires' on 29 Jan 2026. Whether a funded initiative exists or this remains exploratory is unknown. 101-day silence suggests no urgency.
No champion. Elaine and Sumesh showed interest but no advocacy behavior. No internal sponsor to drive evaluation forward. Deal stalled because no one is selling for Zycus internally.

No quantified business case or ROI. Pain articulated ('not always the most elegant') but not quantified (no cost, cycle-time, FTE burden). No financial pain owner identified. Without a business case, no EB will fund this.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal went cold 101 days ago with no buyer engagement, no follow-up, no inbound requests, and all four Zycus commitments undelivered. Confidence is zero. The only positive signal is the 29 Jan 2026 discovery call showed genuine interest (Elaine's evaluation criteria, Sumesh's request for materials), but the 101-day silence and execution failure erased it.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jan 2026, qualified same day. Days to close: 264 (close 2027-03-17). Time in Qualified stage: 148 days. Last verified buyer activity: 17 Mar 2026 (101 days ago). Cold for over 100 days forces Off Track verdict regardless of stage. The close date is 9 months out but irrelevant when the deal has been silent for over three months with no forward movement.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has been dead for 101 days since last buyer activity on 17 Mar 2026. Single discovery call on 29 Jan led to four Zycus commitments (Gartner report, NDA, demo, materials)—none delivered. No reschedule after 23 Feb cancellation. Zero forward.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is dark for 101 days with zero buyer-side contact roles, no Economic Buyer mapped, no champion, and all four immediate commitments undelivered. Re-engagement is the only path forward.

competitive_position.summary: No active competitor identified. Coursera uses Coupa today for procure-to-pay. Elaine Gu deflected questions about Coupa contract status and renewal on 29 Jan 2026, saying 'can't share other things yet,' signaling procurement sensitivity or undisclosed evaluation activity. Whether Zycus is positioned as a Coupa replacement, a coexistence play (Zycus intake/CLM + Coupa P2P), or a future option is unknown. The 101-day silence may indicate Coupa renewal closed the window, or that integration/change-management complexity stalled the evaluation. No other vendor named. The competitive landscape is a knowledge gap, not a clear field. Primary current threat: status quo and Coupa incumbency, particularly if Coupa contract was renewed in the interim and the window closed.

competitors.name: Coupa (incumbent)
competitors.quote: You said you're currently using Coupa? Yes. Okay, and is contract coming up or you know, is there a little Coupa fatigue there, or what's kinda got you open open minded about it? ... I don't think we have any. There's can't share other things yet.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus wins on unified intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration with AI-driven Merlin automation and native Teams/Slack integration, versus Coupa's modular, less-integrated architecture. The wedge: demonstrate the end-to-end workflow Elaine requested (intake → contract → PO generation) in a single platform with zero friction, and quantify the time/cost savings versus Coupa's current fragmented process. Activation: deliver the overdue demo immediately, show the orchestration live, and build a business case that makes Coupa's renewal decision a mistake.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
competitors.quote: Is there a project going on, or are you just kicking the tires? ... Kicking the tires. We have systems now, ways of doing things, but it's not always the most elegant.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Sumesh Sekaran and Emily Karjala
competitors.how_we_win: Convert qualitative pain ('not always the most elegant') into quantified cost of inaction. Show Coursera the FTE burden, cycle-time delays, and maverick spend leakage caused by fragmented workflows, and tie it to a business outcome (e.g., 30% faster contract-to-PO cycle, 15% procurement cost reduction) the Economic Buyer would fund. The 101-day silence suggests status quo won Round 1; re-engaging requires proving the pain is urgent and solvable.

items.criterion: ERP integration capability (ERP system undisclosed, NDA required)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Coursera's stated priorities align well with Zycus's core strengths (intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration, AI automation, Teams/Slack integration, unified platform), but fit is unvalidated because the demo never happened. The deal stalled in pre-validation stage.

hard.ais_why:
• The 19-minute intro covered Zycus's 'AI-driven automation' pitch, and Coursera's team asked how intake integrates with purchase requests via Teams or Slack - interest in orchestration, not specifically AI.
• Coursera flagged misalignment on the call purpose (expected a demo) and is gating ERP details behind an NDA; questions stayed mechanical rather than aspirational on AI.
• One recorded intro plus a cancelled follow-up; next demo deferred to March - engagement is steady but AI excitement is Zycus-led, so Curious.
hard.next_step:
• Send Gartner Magic Quadrant report
  • Send NDA
  • Arrange demo session in March
  • Provide post-call reading materials for review

deltas.to: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: This deal went cold 101 days ago (last touch 17 Mar 2026) after a single discovery call on 29 Jan 2026. All four commitments made on that
deltas.subject: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: This deal went cold 101 days ago (last touch 17 Mar 2026) after a single discovery call on 29 Jan 2026. All four commitments made on that call (Gartner report, NDA, demo, reading materials) remain undelivered. Re-engage Sumesh Sekaran and Elaine Gu with a direct acknowledgment of the dropped ball: apologize for the delay, deliver all four commitments in one package, and ask explicitly whether Coursera still has interest and budget for a procurement workflow initiative. If yes, schedule the demo Elaine requested (intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration) within 7 days. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to executive connect (Shekhar Varma engaging the relevant stakeholder) to assess whether the opportunity is salvageable or should be disqualified.

deltas.to: MAP THE ECONOMIC BUYER: 148 days in Qualified stage with no Economic Buyer identified and no budget conversation is a disqualification risk. The $130k deal size
deltas.subject: MAP THE ECONOMIC BUYER: 148 days in Qualified stage with no Economic Buyer identified and no budget conversation is a disqualification risk. The $130k deal size and enterprise procurement scope suggest COO Shravan Goli or the CFO as likely Economic Buyers, but neither has been engaged. Upon re-engagement, ask Sumesh and Elaine directly: 'Who controls the budget for a procurement platform initiative? Who would need to approve a $130k investment?' Then secure a meeting with that person to validate business case and commercial feasibility. Do not proceed to demo without EB mapping.

deltas.to: DEVELOP A CHAMPION OR DISQUALIFY: No champion exists. Elaine Gu showed interest but went silent; Sumesh Sekaran said he was 'kicking the tires' and has not re-e
deltas.subject: DEVELOP A CHAMPION OR DISQUALIFY: No champion exists. Elaine Gu showed interest but went silent; Sumesh Sekaran said he was 'kicking the tires' and has not re-engaged. Upon re-engagement, assess whether Elaine or Sumesh can be developed into a champion by arming them with a quantified business case (FTE savings, cycle-time reduction, ROI model) and securing their commitment to introduce the Economic Buyer and drive the evaluation. If neither will advocate internally, this deal lacks the internal sponsorship to close and should be disqualified or downgraded to nurture.

value.name: Coupa (incumbent)
value.quote: You said you're currently using Coupa? Yes. Okay, and is contract coming up or you know, is there a little Coupa fatigue there, or what's kinda got you open open minded about it? ... I don't think we have any. There's can't share other things yet.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Elaine Gu
value.how_we_win: Zycus wins on unified intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration with AI-driven Merlin automation and native Teams/Slack integration, versus Coupa's modular, less-integrated architecture. The wedge: demonstrate the end-to-end workflow Elaine requested (intake → contract → PO generation) in a single platform with zero friction, and quantify the time/cost savings versus Coupa's current fragmented process. Activation: deliver the overdue demo immediately, show the orchestration live, and build a business case that makes Coupa's renewal decision a mistake.

packets.subject: Coupa (incumbent)

value.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
value.quote: Is there a project going on, or are you just kicking the tires? ... Kicking the tires. We have systems now, ways of doing things, but it's not always the most elegant.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Jan 2026, Sumesh Sekaran and Emily Karjala
value.how_we_win: Convert qualitative pain ('not always the most elegant') into quantified cost of inaction. Show Coursera the FTE burden, cycle-time delays, and maverick spend leakage caused by fragmented workflows, and tie it to a business outcome (e.g., 30% faster contract-to-PO cycle, 15% procurement cost reduction) the Economic Buyer would fund. The 101-day silence suggests status quo won Round 1; re-engaging requires proving the pain is urgent and solvable.

packets.subject: Do Nothing / Status Quo

All four commitments made on that call (Gartner report, NDA, demo, reading materials) remain undelivered. Re-engage Sumesh Sekaran and Elaine Gu with a direct acknowledgment of the dropped ball: apologize for the delay, deliver all four commitments in one package, and ask explicitly whether Coursera still has interest and budget for a procurement workflow initiative. If yes, schedule the demo Elaine requested (intake-to-contract-to-PO orchestration) within 7 days. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to executive connect (Shekhar Varma engaging the relevant stakeholder) to assess whether the opportunity is salvageable or should be disqualified.

gaps: Only 1 transcribed buyer call (29 Jan 2026 discovery); second meeting (23 Feb 2026) cancelled with no reschedule, blocking deeper discovery
No AIS score or AI excitement signal populated in Salesforce despite Merlin products in scope
No field history for stage, amount, close date, or forecast (opportunity likely created and qualified same day with no subsequent changes)
Zero OpportunityContactRole buyer-side entries despite three confirmed participants on 29 Jan call (hygiene gap)
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion contact roles; no EB or DM engaged across calls or tasks
No completed tasks evidencing delivery of the four commitments (Gartner report, NDA, demo, reading materials)
ERP system undisclosed (NDA required), blocking integration fit assessment
Coupa contract status and competitive landscape unknown (Elaine deflected question)
Decision process, procurement timeline, and project status unknown (Sumesh said 'kicking the tires', no formal project confirmed)
